tax: Placed at genus rank, Choke-Wind is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a foul settled-air body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a heavy foul-air body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by an invisible flame-killing pool. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a dying flame in still air — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Choke-Wind occupies low still dips. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure animates a foul-gas pocket and recur with the foul-gas seep refills, sustaining themselves on heavy foul gas.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: pools low and silently suffocates. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: foul-air body and motion-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: suffocation and downhill following. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Choke-Wind matters because silent killer of low galleries. It lets survey reports sort pooling foul-air forms from spinning ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Choke-Wind is the genus-rank grouping of foul settled-air elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a heavy foul-air body that suffocates without a sound, and divide below into species groups by gas type and pooling depth. An invisible pool of un-breathable air.
